metadata.title: Build a Criteria-First Prompt Generator with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Namaste, Liam — in for Bear. How does build a criteria-first prompt generator with claude? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Build a Criteria-First Prompt Generator with Claude?
props.segment: Build a Criteria-First Prompt Generator with

lines: Put the test before the task.

beats.label: Hook — criteria last
beats.narration: The single highest-leverage change to most prompts is putting the evaluation criteria first. Not after the task — before it. Most people have never tried it.

shots.notes: Two-panel split: LEFT = Standard prompt order: Task -> Context -> Output. RIGHT = Criteria-first order: Criteria -> Task -> Context -> Output. Arrow pointing right labeled 'higher leverage'. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Claude command
beats.narration: The seed command takes any natural-language request and asks Claude to rewrite it criteria-first — leading with testable evaluation criteria before stating the task.

props.command: claude "Rewrite this request as a criteria-first prompt: lead with evaluation criteria (how to judge the output), then state what to produce. Criteria must be specific and testable — 'under 100 words' yes, 'good' no." < request.txt

beats.label: Python code
beats.narration: The script runs both versions — standard and criteria-first — through Claude, then shows the outputs side by side so you can see what moved.

props.code:
import subprocess, sys
request = open(sys.argv[1]).read()
rewrite_cmd = (
  "Rewrite as criteria-first prompt: lead with "
  "testable evaluation criteria, then the task."
)
standard_out = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", request], capture_output=True, text=True
).stdout
criteria_out = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", rewrite_cmd], input=request,
  capture_output=True, text=True
).stdout
print("STANDARD:\n", standard_out)
print("CRITERIA-FIRST:\n", criteria_out)

beats.label: Side-by-side comparison
beats.narration: The criteria-first version shifts Claude's attention from 'what is this task typically like' to 'what does this specific output need to be.' That's the difference between generating and targeting.

shots.notes: Two-column output comparison. LEFT: Standard prompt output — generic, vague structure. RIGHT: Criteria-first output — tight, criteria-driven. Highlight differences with CRIMSON underlines on the right column where structure tightened.

beats.label: The change — add one criterion
beats.narration: Now add one more criterion to the criteria-first prompt. Watch the output tighten in exactly that dimension — demonstrating that criteria are directly controlling, not just advisory.

props.command: claude "Rewrite with added criterion: no hedging language (no 'might', 'could', 'perhaps')." < criteria_first_prompt.txt

beats.label: Changed output
beats.narration: The hedging language disappears. Not because Claude guessed — because the criterion specified the exact failure mode to avoid.

shots.notes: Before/after output fragment. BEFORE: sentence with 'might suggest that...' AFTER: same sentence rewritten without hedging — direct claim. CRIMSON strikethrough on 'might suggest'. Green checkmark on revised version.

beats.label: Teardown angle
beats.narration: Criteria-first shifts Claude's attention from 'what does this task typically look like' to 'what does this specific output need to be.' That is the difference between generating and targeting.

shots.notes: Large text: 'Criteria-first: from generating to targeting.' Below two rows: GENERATING = 'what is this task usually like?'. TARGETING = 'what must this output be?' Cream background, CRIMSON rule, INK text.

beats.label: Next reel teaser
beats.narration: Next: generate a failure criterion checklist — ask what would make this wrong before it's produced.

shots.notes: Clean: 'NEXT: Failure Criterion Generator →' Minimal teardown style.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Build a Criteria-First Prompt Generator with Claude] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
